---
title: "Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release"
---

# Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release

Range: `1064-1139`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                 | Summary                                                                                                                       |
| ----- | ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1064  | [artifact](#rune-1064)               | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1065  | [assemble {Forge}](#rune-1065)       | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1066  | [baseline {Forge}](#rune-1066)       | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1067  | [binary](#rune-1067)                 | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1068  | [branch {Forge}](#rune-1068)         | A divergence word; execution or history splits into alternate paths.                                                          |
| 1069  | [build](#rune-1069)                  | A forge word; it turns source into artifact.                                                                                  |
| 1070  | [build cache](#rune-1070)            | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1071  | [build graph](#rune-1071)            | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1072  | [bundle](#rune-1072)                 | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1073  | [canary](#rune-1073)                 | A deliberately limited release whose job is to discover whether production reality agrees with the story told in staging.     |
| 1074  | [changelog](#rune-1074)              | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1075  | [cherry-pick](#rune-1075)            | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1076  | [CI](#rune-1076)                     | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1077  | [codegen](#rune-1077)                | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1078  | [commit {Forge}](#rune-1078)         | A sealing word; it makes a change part of durable history.                                                                    |
| 1079  | [compile {Forge}](#rune-1079)        | A transmutation word; it transforms one language into another fit for execution.                                              |
| 1080  | [container {Forge}](#rune-1080)      | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1081  | [container image](#rune-1081)        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1082  | [continuous delivery](#rune-1082)    | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1083  | [continuous integration](#rune-1083) | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1084  | [dependency](#rune-1084)             | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1085  | [dependency graph](#rune-1085)       | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1086  | [deploy](#rune-1086)                 | The crossing from forge to world. Many good ideas become bad facts only after they are actually shipped.                      |
| 1087  | [deployment ring](#rune-1087)        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1088  | [diff {Forge}](#rune-1088)           | A visible account of what changed between one state and another. Good diffs make review local; bad diffs turn truth into fog. |
| 1089  | [distribution](#rune-1089)           | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1090  | [Dockerfile](#rune-1090)             | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1091  | [downgrade](#rune-1091)              | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1092  | [environment](#rune-1092)            | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1093  | [feature flag](#rune-1093)           | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1094  | [formatter](#rune-1094)              | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1095  | [hermetic build](#rune-1095)         | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1096  | [install](#rune-1096)                | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1097  | [installer](#rune-1097)              | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1098  | [issue tracker](#rune-1098)          | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1099  | [job runner](#rune-1099)             | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1100  | [label {Forge}](#rune-1100)          | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1101  | [lint](#rune-1101)                   | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1102  | [lockfile](#rune-1102)               | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1103  | [manifest](#rune-1103)               | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1104  | [merge {Forge}](#rune-1104)          | A reconciliation word; separate lines of change are brought back together.                                                    |
| 1105  | [migration script](#rune-1105)       | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1106  | [minifier](#rune-1106)               | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1107  | [package {Forge}](#rune-1107)        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1108  | [package manager](#rune-1108)        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1109  | [patch release](#rune-1109)          | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1110  | [pin](#rune-1110)                    | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1111  | [pipeline {Forge}](#rune-1111)       | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                                                        |
| 1112  | [precommit hook](#rune-1112)         | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1113  | [preview environment](#rune-1113)    | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1114  | [promote](#rune-1114)                | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1115  | [publish](#rune-1115)                | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1116  | [rebase](#rune-1116)                 | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1117  | [release](#rune-1117)                | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1118  | [release candidate](#rune-1118)      | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1119  | [repository {Forge}](#rune-1119)     | A custody word; an official place from which versions or entities are retrieved.                                              |
| 1120  | [reproducible build](#rune-1120)     | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1121  | [restore {Forge}](#rune-1121)        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1122  | [revision {Forge}](#rune-1122)       | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1123  | [rollback {Forge}](#rune-1123)       | A reversal word; the system returns toward an earlier trusted state.                                                          |
| 1124  | [rollout](#rune-1124)                | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1125  | [scaffold {Forge}](#rune-1125)       | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1126  | [semantic version](#rune-1126)       | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                              |
| 1127  | [ship](#rune-1127)                   | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1128  | [source map](#rune-1128)             | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |
| 1129  | [staging](#rune-1129)                | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1130  | [static analysis](#rune-1130)        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1131  | [tag](#rune-1131)                    | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1132  | [template {Forge}](#rune-1132)       | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1133  | [test gate](#rune-1133)              | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1134  | [toolchain](#rune-1134)              | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1135  | [transitive dependency](#rune-1135)  | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1136  | [upgrade](#rune-1136)                | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                              |
| 1137  | [version](#rune-1137)                | A chronology word; the same thing is named at different moments.                                                              |
| 1138  | [version pin](#rune-1138)            | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                     |
| 1139  | [workspace](#rune-1139)              | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                |

## Entries

### [1064] artifact {#rune-1064}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

**Pocket gloss:** A produced thing you can store, ship, or compare.

### [1065] assemble {Forge} {#rune-1065}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1066] baseline {Forge} {#rune-1066}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1067] binary {#rune-1067}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1068] branch {Forge} {#rune-1068}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A divergence word; execution or history splits into alternate paths.

**Pocket gloss:** A line of history where change can proceed without touching the main trunk.

### [1069] build {#rune-1069}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A forge word; it turns source into artifact.

**Pocket gloss:** Turn source into a runnable or shippable artifact.

### [1070] build cache {#rune-1070}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1071] build graph {#rune-1071}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1072] bundle {#rune-1072}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1073] canary {#rune-1073}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** A deliberately limited release whose job is to discover whether production reality agrees with the story told in staging.

**Pocket gloss:** A small production release meant to detect reality before full rollout.

### [1074] changelog {#rune-1074}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1075] cherry-pick {#rune-1075}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1076] CI {#rune-1076}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

**Pocket gloss:** Continuous integration: change is merged only after automated checks speak.

### [1077] codegen {#rune-1077}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1078] commit {Forge} {#rune-1078}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A sealing word; it makes a change part of durable history.

**Pocket gloss:** Record a change as one named point in history.

### [1079] compile {Forge} {#rune-1079}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A transmutation word; it transforms one language into another fit for execution.

### [1080] container {Forge} {#rune-1080}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1081] container image {#rune-1081}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1082] continuous delivery {#rune-1082}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1083] continuous integration {#rune-1083}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1084] dependency {#rune-1084}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

**Pocket gloss:** Something your code needs in order to build or run.

### [1085] dependency graph {#rune-1085}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1086] deploy {#rune-1086}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** The crossing from forge to world. Many good ideas become bad facts only after they are actually shipped.

**Pocket gloss:** Cross the line from forge to world.

### [1087] deployment ring {#rune-1087}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1088] diff {Forge} {#rune-1088}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** A visible account of what changed between one state and another. Good diffs make review local; bad diffs turn truth into fog.

**Pocket gloss:** A visible account of what changed between states.

### [1089] distribution {#rune-1089}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1090] Dockerfile {#rune-1090}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1091] downgrade {#rune-1091}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1092] environment {#rune-1092}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1093] feature flag {#rune-1093}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

**Pocket gloss:** A runtime switch that separates deploy from release.

### [1094] formatter {#rune-1094}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1095] hermetic build {#rune-1095}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1096] install {#rune-1096}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1097] installer {#rune-1097}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1098] issue tracker {#rune-1098}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1099] job runner {#rune-1099}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1100] label {Forge} {#rune-1100}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1101] lint {#rune-1101}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1102] lockfile {#rune-1102}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

**Pocket gloss:** The file that freezes dependency resolution into repeatable history.

### [1103] manifest {#rune-1103}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1104] merge {Forge} {#rune-1104}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A reconciliation word; separate lines of change are brought back together.

**Pocket gloss:** Reconcile divergent lines of history into one.

### [1105] migration script {#rune-1105}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1106] minifier {#rune-1106}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1107] package {Forge} {#rune-1107}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1108] package manager {#rune-1108}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1109] patch release {#rune-1109}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1110] pin {#rune-1110}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1111] pipeline {Forge} {#rune-1111}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.

**Pocket gloss:** The ordered automation that turns edit into tested artifact.

### [1112] precommit hook {#rune-1112}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1113] preview environment {#rune-1113}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1114] promote {#rune-1114}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1115] publish {#rune-1115}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1116] rebase {#rune-1116}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1117] release {#rune-1117}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

**Pocket gloss:** A named version intended for actual users.

### [1118] release candidate {#rune-1118}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1119] repository {Forge} {#rune-1119}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A custody word; an official place from which versions or entities are retrieved.

### [1120] reproducible build {#rune-1120}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

**Pocket gloss:** A build that can be rebuilt into the same bits again.

### [1121] restore {Forge} {#rune-1121}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1122] revision {Forge} {#rune-1122}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1123] rollback {Forge} {#rune-1123}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A reversal word; the system returns toward an earlier trusted state.

**Pocket gloss:** Operational permission to retreat after release.

### [1124] rollout {#rune-1124}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1125] scaffold {Forge} {#rune-1125}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1126] semantic version {#rune-1126}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.

### [1127] ship {#rune-1127}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1128] source map {#rune-1128}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.

### [1129] staging {#rune-1129}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1130] static analysis {#rune-1130}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1131] tag {#rune-1131}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1132] template {Forge} {#rune-1132}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1133] test gate {#rune-1133}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1134] toolchain {#rune-1134}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1135] transitive dependency {#rune-1135}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1136] upgrade {#rune-1136}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.

### [1137] version {#rune-1137}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A chronology word; the same thing is named at different moments.

### [1138] version pin {#rune-1138}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.

### [1139] workspace {#rune-1139}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.
